qa.aEn: **Hooks (lifecycle hooks)** let you inject custom automation logic at key points in Claude Code. Imagine installing inspection stations along a factory conveyor belt: each time a product passes a station, an automatic check fires. Claude Code's Hooks are those inspection stations — you pre-configure "when event X fires, run command Y." Claude Code supports 27 Hook events covering key moments in AI behavior: **PreToolUse** (before a tool call), **PostToolUse** (after a tool call), **PostSampling** (after every AI reply completes), **SessionStart** (session begins), **UserTurn** (after user input), and more. Practical examples: set a Hook so Claude automatically runs lint after every file edit (PostToolUse) and immediately tells Claude about formatting issues; or have the Bash command content emailed to you before Claude runs any Bash command (PreToolUse); or automatically inject the latest Git status into context at session start (SessionStart). Especially powerful is the **exit code 2 blocking semantic**: if your Hook script returns exit code 2, Claude Code pauses and waits for user confirmation instead of continuing. This makes Hooks a human-approval checkpoint.

qa.q: Skills 和普通的 CLAUDE.md 指令有什么区别？
qa.qEn: What's the difference between Skills and regular CLAUDE.md instructions?
qa.a: CLAUDE.md 是你写给 Claude 的静态说明文档，Claude 把它当作背景知识来理解，但执行时还是依靠 AI 的判断。Skills 是更主动的触发机制——一个 Skill 有明确的 `whenToUse` 描述，AI 在对话中会自动判断"当前情况是否匹配这个 Skill 的使用场景"，如果匹配就自动触发，不需要用户显式输入命令。类比：CLAUDE.md 像"员工手册"——员工知道了规定，但还要靠自己判断每个情况如何应对；Skills 像"标准操作程序"（SOP）——特定触发条件下，自动执行预设流程，无需额外判断。例如：你写一个 Skill 叫"代码审查"，`whenToUse` 描述是"当用户提交新功能代码时"，Skill 内容是一个详细的代码审查检查表。以后只要用户说"我刚写了个新功能"，Claude 会自动触发这个 Skill 来执行代码审查，而不需要用户记住要输入 `/code-review`。这种 AI 自主触发的能力在竞品中几乎没有对等设计——Cursor 有斜杠命令，但需要用户显式触发；Claude Code 的 Skills 让 AI 自己判断时机。
qa.aEn: CLAUDE.md is a static instruction document you write for Claude — Claude treats it as background knowledge but still relies on AI judgment at execution time. **Skills are a more proactive trigger mechanism** — each Skill has an explicit `whenToUse` description, and the AI automatically judges during a conversation whether the current situation matches that Skill's scenario. If it matches, the Skill auto-fires without the user typing a command. Analogy: CLAUDE.md is the "employee handbook" — the employee knows the rules but has to judge each situation themselves. Skills are "Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)" — under specific triggering conditions, a preset procedure executes automatically without extra judgment. Example: write a Skill called "code review" with `whenToUse` = "when the user submits new feature code," and the Skill body is a detailed review checklist. From then on, whenever the user says "I just wrote a new feature," Claude auto-fires the Skill to perform the review — the user doesn't need to remember to type `/code-review`. This AI-autonomous-triggering capability has almost no equivalent in competitors — Cursor has slash commands, but requires explicit user invocation. Claude Code's Skills let the AI judge the timing.

qa.q: MCP 服务器在外部进程运行，Claude Code 怎么保证它们不是恶意的？允许任意 MCP 服务器连接不是很危险吗？
qa.qEn: MCP Servers run in external processes — how does Claude Code ensure they're not malicious? Isn't allowing any MCP Server to connect dangerous?
qa.a: 这是一个合理的安全关切。MCP Server 确实在外部进程（甚至远程机器）上运行，Claude Code 对其结果无法直接验证。Claude Code 有几道防线。企业策略控制：企业管理员可以通过策略配置"只允许特定的 MCP Server"，禁止员工连接未经审批的 Server。权限检查透明化：MCP 工具以 `mcp__服务器名__工具名` 的格式出现，让用户清楚地知道这个工具来自哪个外部服务器，不是内置工具。结果不信任原则：MCP 工具的执行结果被当作外部数据，会经过 Claude Code 的内容审查，不会被当作可信指令直接执行。沙箱限制网络出口：即使恶意 MCP Server 试图让 Claude 执行危险操作，沙箱可以限制网络访问范围。但诚实说：MCP 的信任模型本质上是"用户信任 MCP Server 的作者"。就像你信任 npm 包的作者一样。如果你连接了一个恶意 MCP Server，它可以返回精心构造的内容来进行提示词注入。对用户的建议：只连接来源可信的 MCP Server（官方发布或信任的开源项目），不要随意配置来历不明的 MCP Server，就像不应该随意运行来历不明的 npm 包。
qa.aEn: A legitimate security concern. MCP Servers indeed run in external processes (possibly on remote machines), and Claude Code can't directly verify their results. Several defenses. **Enterprise-policy control**: admins can configure "only these specific MCP Servers are allowed," blocking employees from connecting to unapproved servers. **Transparent permission checks**: MCP tools appear as `mcp__serverName__toolName`, making it obvious which external server a tool came from and that it's not built-in. **Don't-trust-results principle**: MCP tool results are treated as external data and go through Claude Code's content review — they're not executed as trusted instructions. **Sandbox limits network egress**: even if a malicious MCP Server tries to make Claude perform dangerous operations, the sandbox can limit network reach. But honestly: MCP's trust model is fundamentally "the user trusts the MCP Server's author," just like you trust npm package authors. If you connect a malicious MCP Server, it can return carefully crafted content to perform prompt injection. User advice: only connect MCP Servers from trusted sources (official releases or reputable open-source projects) — don't casually configure servers of unknown provenance, the same way you shouldn't casually run unknown npm packages.

qa.q: Token 是什么？为什么会"花钱"？
qa.qEn: What are tokens? Why do they cost money?
qa.a: Token 是大语言模型处理文本的基本单位——模型不是以"字"或"字母"为单位处理文本，而是以 Token 为单位。英文一个 Token 大约对应 4 个字母或 0.75 个单词；中文一个汉字通常对应 1-2 个 Token（取决于具体的分词器）。Anthropic 的 API 按 Token 计费，分四种价格：输入 Token（你发送给 AI 的内容）、输出 Token（AI 生成的内容，贵 5 倍）、缓存写入 Token（稍贵于普通输入，是一次性投资）、缓存读取 Token（比普通输入便宜 10 倍）。为什么输出比输入贵？因为"生成"比"理解"计算量更大——模型理解你的输入相对快，但生成每一个新 Token 都需要独立的前向计算。实际使用感受：一次普通的"帮我解释这段代码"对话可能消耗几千 Token；让 Claude 对一个大型代码库做全面分析，可能消耗几十万 Token，产生几美元甚至十几美元的费用。这就是为什么 Claude Code 要做各种压缩和缓存优化——每节省一个 Token，都是真实的成本节省。
qa.aEn: **Tokens** are the basic unit LLMs use to process text — the model doesn't process text character-by-character or letter-by-letter, but token-by-token. One English token corresponds to roughly 4 letters or 0.75 words; one Chinese character is typically 1-2 tokens (depending on the tokenizer). The Anthropic API bills by tokens at four prices: **input tokens** (what you send to the AI), **output tokens** (what the AI generates — 5x more expensive), **cache-write tokens** (slightly more than normal input, a one-time investment), **cache-read tokens** (10x cheaper than normal input). Why are output tokens more expensive than input? Because "generation" takes more compute than "understanding" — the model understands your input relatively quickly, but generating each new token requires an independent forward pass. In practice: a normal "explain this code" conversation might consume a few thousand tokens; asking Claude to do a full analysis of a large codebase might consume hundreds of thousands of tokens, producing a cost of a few dollars to over ten dollars. That's why Claude Code does so much compression and caching optimization — every saved token is a real cost saving.

qa.q: Prompt Cache 怎么工作？我能从中省多少钱？
qa.qEn: How does Prompt Cache work? How much money can I save with it?
qa.a: Prompt Cache 是 Anthropic API 的一个定价优惠机制：如果两次请求的消息前缀相同（逐 Token 比较），重复的部分按"缓存读取"计费，价格是普通输入的十分之一。系统提示词（角色定义 + 工具描述 + CLAUDE.md）每次请求都会发送，而且几乎不变——这就是最大的缓存收益来源。举例：假设你的系统提示词有 20,000 Token，一次普通对话你还发送了 5,000 Token 的历史消息和 500 Token 的新提问。不用缓存：25,500 Token 按输入价格计费。用缓存且命中：20,000 Token 按缓存读取价格（1/10）+ 5,500 Token 按普通输入价格。节省约 20,000 * (1 - 0.1) = 节省 18,000 Token 的费用，相当于省了总费用的 70%。要让缓存命中，关键是消息前缀稳定。Claude Code 通过仔细设计系统提示词的排列顺序（稳定内容在前，易变内容在后）来最大化缓存命中率。对于频繁使用 Claude Code 的开发者，Prompt Cache 可以把 API 费用降低 50-80%，是非常显著的成本优化。
qa.aEn: **Prompt Cache** is a pricing-discount mechanism on the Anthropic API: if two requests share an identical message prefix (compared token-by-token), the duplicate portion bills as "cache read" at one-tenth the normal input price. The System Prompt (role definition + tool descriptions + CLAUDE.md) is sent on every request and barely changes — it's the biggest cache win. Example: suppose your System Prompt is 20,000 tokens, and on a normal turn you also send 5,000 tokens of history and a 500-token new question. **Without cache**: 25,500 tokens at input price. **With cache hit**: 20,000 tokens at cache-read price (1/10) + 5,500 tokens at normal input price. You save about 20,000 × (1 - 0.1) = 18,000 tokens' worth of cost — roughly 70% of the total. The key to cache hits is prefix stability. Claude Code maximizes hit rate by carefully ordering the System Prompt (stable content first, volatile content last). For frequent Claude Code users, Prompt Cache can cut API costs by 50-80% — a very significant optimization.

qa.q: Context Window（上下文窗口）是什么概念？满了会怎样？
qa.qEn: What exactly is the Context Window? What happens when it fills up?
qa.a: Context Window 是 AI 模型在一次请求中能处理的最大 Token 数量，相当于 AI 的"工作记忆"容量。Claude 3.x 的 Context Window 上限约为 200,000 Token（约 15 万英文单词，或者 10-15 本普通小说的篇幅）。这看起来很大，但在实际使用中会很快被填满：代码库的关键文件读取（几千 Token 每个文件）+ 对话历史 + 系统提示词 + 工具调用结果，加起来很容易到 10 万 Token，尤其是大型项目的深度分析。满了会怎样？Claude Code 有五层压缩机制来推迟"满"这一刻：裁剪超大工具结果、压缩旧的对话历史、折叠不重要的中间步骤、最终用 AI 生成对话摘要替换原文。如果压缩后还是满了，查询循环会触发 `blocking_limit` 退出，停止继续处理，提示用户上下文已满。用户这时候通常需要开启一个新对话，或者用 `/compact` 命令手动触发压缩。这是目前大语言模型的根本限制，不是 Claude Code 的设计缺陷——所有基于 LLM 的系统都面临这个问题。
qa.aEn: The **Context Window** is the maximum number of tokens an AI model can process in one request — effectively the AI's "working memory" capacity. Claude 3.x's Context Window cap is about 200,000 tokens (roughly 150,000 English words, or 10-15 typical novels). That looks huge, but it fills up fast in real use: reading key files from a codebase (several thousand tokens per file) + conversation history + System Prompt + tool results can easily reach 100,000 tokens, especially during deep analysis of a large project. **What happens when it fills up?** Claude Code has a five-tier compression pipeline to defer the "full" moment: trim oversize tool results, compress old conversation history, fold unimportant intermediate steps, and finally have the AI generate a summary to replace the original. If it's still full after compression, the query loop triggers a `blocking_limit` exit — processing stops and the user is told the context is full. At that point, the user usually needs to start a new conversation or run `/compact` to manually compress. This is a fundamental LLM limit, not a Claude Code design flaw — every LLM-based system faces it.

qa.q: 投机执行和 Prompt Cache 加在一起，Claude Code 能有多高效？有实际的数据吗？
qa.qEn: Combining speculative execution and Prompt Cache, how efficient is Claude Code? Are there real numbers?
qa.a: 具体的性能数据 Anthropic 没有公开，但可以从代码设计推算各个优化的理论上限。Prompt Cache 的效果是最显著和可量化的：如果系统提示词占每次请求的 60-70%（这在实际使用中很常见），缓存命中可以把每次请求的输入 Token 成本降低 50-65%。这不是理论数字——这是直接可以在账单上看到的节省。投机执行的效果更难量化：它减少的是"等待时间"而不是"Token 消耗"。在 AI 流式回复时同时开始执行只读工具，理论上可以把"AI 生成时间 + 工具执行时间"变成并行的，总时间接近两者中的较大值。实际效果取决于工具执行时间和 AI 生成时间的比例，估计可以减少 20-30% 的总响应时间。上下文压缩的效果是"让任务能继续进行"，很难用百分比量化，但对于长时间运行的大任务（重构整个代码库），没有压缩机制会话会在中途强制终止，有了压缩才能跑完。综合来看，Claude Code 的 Token 经济学优化是真实有效的，但具体数字取决于你的使用场景——频繁、长会话的用户收益最大。
qa.aEn: Anthropic hasn't published specific performance numbers, but we can estimate theoretical upper bounds from the code design. **Prompt Cache's effect is the most significant and quantifiable**: if the System Prompt is 60-70% of each request (very common in practice), a cache hit cuts each request's input-token cost by 50-65%. Not theoretical — this shows up directly on your bill. **Speculative execution's effect is harder to quantify**: it reduces "wait time" rather than "token consumption." Starting read-only tool execution during AI streaming theoretically overlaps "AI generation time + tool execution time," making the total close to the larger of the two. Actual gains depend on the ratio of tool time to generation time — estimated 20-30% reduction in total response time. **Context compression's effect is "letting the task continue"** — hard to quantify as a percentage, but for long-running big tasks (refactoring a whole codebase), sessions would forcibly terminate midway without compression, and only with compression can they finish. Taken together, Claude Code's token-economy optimizations are genuinely effective, but the exact numbers depend on your use case — frequent, long-session users benefit the most.

qa.q: Token 计费模式是不是在鼓励 Claude Code 做更多不必要的工具调用，从而产生更多费用？这不是利益冲突吗？
qa.qEn: Doesn't the token billing model incentivize Claude Code to make unnecessary tool calls to generate more fees? Isn't that a conflict of interest?
qa.a: 这是一个聪明的问题，指向了 AI 工具商业模式中的潜在张力。从技术设计角度看，Claude Code 有几个明确的机制来约束不必要的工具调用：最大轮次限制（max_turns）防止无限循环；工具结果大小上限（maxResultSizeChars）限制每次工具调用能消耗的 Token；上下文压缩机制在 Token 积累过多时主动减少消耗；系统提示词明确指示 Claude 优先使用轻量级工具（Grep 替代 Read 全文搜索）。从激励结构角度看：Claude Code 不直接从 Token 消耗中获益——它按固定订阅或 Anthropic 账户计费，多消耗 Token 意味着用户成本上升，可能导致用户减少使用，这对 Anthropic 不利。所以理论上 Anthropic 有激励让每次任务消耗尽量少的 Token，同时提供更好的结果。但这个分析有一个前提：Anthropic 的收入模型确实是"让用户长期持续使用"，而不是"短期尽量多计费"。对于企业订阅用户，这个对齐更明显；对于按量付费的 API 用户，激励结构确实存在一定张力。用户有权利关注自己的 Token 消耗，Claude Code 也提供了 `/usage` 命令查看当前会话的消耗。
qa.aEn: A sharp question pointing at a real tension in AI-tool business models. **From a technical-design angle**, Claude Code has several explicit constraints against unnecessary tool calls: max-turns prevents infinite loops; `maxResultSizeChars` caps how many tokens one tool call can consume; context compression actively reduces consumption once tokens pile up; the System Prompt explicitly instructs Claude to prefer lightweight tools (Grep over reading whole files). **From an incentive-structure angle**: Claude Code doesn't directly profit from token consumption — it's billed by fixed subscription or Anthropic account. More token use means higher user cost, which may reduce usage, which hurts Anthropic. So Anthropic theoretically has an incentive to finish each task with as few tokens as possible while delivering better results. But this analysis assumes Anthropic's revenue model is "keep users using long-term" rather than "bill as much as possible short-term." For enterprise subscription users this alignment is clearer; for pay-as-you-go API users some tension genuinely exists. Users have every right to watch their own token consumption — Claude Code provides a `/usage` command to check the current session's usage.

qa.q: Claude Code 有九层配置，我作为普通用户平时接触的是哪几层？
qa.qEn: Claude Code has nine configuration layers — as a regular user, which ones do I actually touch?
qa.a: 普通用户日常接触的主要是三层，从最常用到最少用。第一层（最常用）：用户全局设置，存储在 `~/.claude/settings.json`。这是你的个人偏好——比如默认模型、主题、是否开启某些功能。你在 Claude Code 界面里改的大多数设置都写在这里。第二层（项目相关）：项目级设置（`.claude/settings.json`）和个人私有设置（`.claude/settings.local.json`）。前者是和团队共享的项目规范（提交到 Git），后者是你对这个项目的个人调整（不提交 Git）。第三层（较少接触）：CLAUDE.md 文件，虽然不是"settings.json"，但在配置治理里扮演重要角色——它是项目的 AI 行为指令。企业用户还会接触企业策略层（MDM 远程下发），但这通常由 IT 部门管理，普通员工感知不到，只会在设置里看到"由管理员管理"的灰色选项。九层听起来复杂，但设计的核心是让配置"就近生效"——你改个人偏好不影响项目，项目规范不影响其他同事的个人设置，企业策略对所有人统一生效。
qa.aEn: A regular user typically touches three layers, from most to least frequent. **Layer 1 (most used)**: user-global settings in `~/.claude/settings.json`. These are your personal preferences — default model, theme, feature toggles. Most of the changes you make in the Claude Code UI land here. **Layer 2 (project-related)**: project settings (`.claude/settings.json`) and personal-per-project settings (`.claude/settings.local.json`). The former is the team-shared project convention (committed to Git); the latter is your personal adjustment for this project (not committed). **Layer 3 (less common)**: CLAUDE.md — not a settings.json, but it plays an important role in configuration governance as the project's AI-behavior instructions. Enterprise users also encounter the enterprise-policy layer (MDM-pushed), but it's typically managed by IT, invisible to regular employees aside from seeing "managed by admin" greyed-out options. Nine layers sounds complex, but the design's core idea is **proximity** — personal preferences don't affect the project, project conventions don't affect coworkers' personal settings, and enterprise policy applies uniformly.

qa.q: `.claude/settings.json` 和 `.claude/settings.local.json` 有什么区别？什么该写在哪里？
qa.qEn: What's the difference between `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json`? What should go where?
qa.a: 两者的核心区别是"是否提交到 Git"，决定了"信息是否对团队可见"。`settings.json`（提交 Git，团队共享）：放这里的配置代表"这个项目的统一规范"——所有团队成员克隆代码库后都会得到这些配置。适合放：团队要求的统一工具白名单（`allowedTools`）、项目级的 Hooks 配置（比如"每次提交前运行 lint"）、团队统一的 MCP Server 配置（比如"这个项目连接内部数据库"）。`settings.local.json`（不提交 Git，仅本地生效）：放这里的配置代表"我自己对这个项目的个人调整"，不会影响其他人。适合放：你个人喜欢的快捷键绑定、你的私人 MCP Server 配置（其他人没有访问权限）、你在这个项目里的个人偏好（比如"不要显示某些提示"）。黄金规则：如果这个配置应该对所有使用这个项目的人生效，放 `settings.json`；如果只想对自己生效，放 `settings.local.json`。和 Git 的 `.gitignore` 类比：`.claude/settings.local.json` 本身应该被加入到 `.gitignore` 里。
qa.aEn: The key difference is "committed to Git or not," which decides "visible to the team or not." **`settings.json` (committed, team-shared)**: put "the project's unified convention" here — every team member gets these settings when they clone the repo. Good for: the team's shared tool allowlist (`allowedTools`), project-level Hooks config ("run lint before every commit"), team-shared MCP Server config ("this project connects to our internal database"). **`settings.local.json` (not committed, local only)**: put "my personal adjustment for this project" here — it doesn't affect anyone else. Good for: your personal keybindings, private MCP Server config (others don't have access anyway), your personal preferences for this project ("don't show certain prompts"). Golden rule: if the setting should apply to everyone using the project, put it in `settings.json`; if it's just for you, put it in `settings.local.json`. Git analogy: `.claude/settings.local.json` itself should be added to `.gitignore`.

qa.q: 配置的合并规则是怎样的？如果多个层级配置了相同的东西，谁说了算？
qa.qEn: How do configs merge? If multiple layers set the same thing, who wins?
qa.a: 配置合并遵循一条核心原则：**高优先级层的配置覆盖低优先级层**，企业策略层永远最高。优先级从高到低：Policy Settings（企业强制）→ Flag Settings（功能开关）→ Enterprise Settings（企业配置文件）→ Managed Settings（托管设置）→ Project Settings → Local Project Settings → User Settings → Default Settings。对于简单值（字符串、布尔）：高优先级直接覆盖低优先级。比如你在用户设置里设置了"模型 = claude-3-sonnet"，但企业策略指定了"模型 = claude-3-haiku"，最终用 haiku。对于数组值（比如 `allowedTools`）：这里有反直觉的设计——**不同来源的数组会被合并（拼接+去重）**，而不是互相覆盖。这意味着企业策略添加的工具限制会和项目设置的工具列表合并，而不是替换它。这样设计是为了安全：管理员可以在企业层"追加"限制，而不会意外删除项目层设置的重要配置。同一来源内的多个文件：后面的文件覆盖前面的文件（覆盖语义）。
qa.aEn: Config merging follows one core principle: **higher-priority layers override lower-priority layers**, with enterprise policy always at the top. Priority high-to-low: Policy Settings (enterprise-forced) → Flag Settings (feature flags) → Enterprise Settings (enterprise config files) → Managed Settings → Project Settings → Local Project Settings → User Settings → Default Settings. For **scalar values** (strings, booleans), higher priority directly overrides lower. If your user settings say "model = claude-3-sonnet" but enterprise policy says "model = claude-3-haiku," haiku wins. For **array values** (like `allowedTools`), there's a counterintuitive design — **arrays from different sources are merged (concatenate + deduplicate)**, not overridden. This means enterprise-policy tool restrictions merge with project-settings tool lists rather than replacing them. The reason is safety: the admin can *append* restrictions at the enterprise layer without accidentally removing important project-layer settings. Within the same source, multiple files: later files override earlier (override semantics).

qa.q: 九层配置是不是过度设计了？对于大多数用户（个人开发者），这些复杂性是多余的负担吗？
qa.qEn: Is nine-layer config over-engineered? For most users (solo developers), is this complexity extra baggage?
qa.a: 这个质疑有道理，值得正面回应。对个人开发者而言，九层配置中有五层（Policy Settings 的四个子层 + Feature Flag）基本是透明不可见的，不需要关心。真正需要配置的通常只有两三个文件：`~/.claude/settings.json`（全局偏好）、`.claude/settings.json`（项目配置）、`CLAUDE.md`（AI 行为指令）。所以复杂性对个人用户的实际影响有限——你可以永远只用三层，其他层对你不可见。但这九层不是"为个人用户设计的"，而是"为企业用户不可或缺的"。企业场景里：IT 部门要能强制安全策略（不能被员工绕过）、团队要能共享项目规范（不用每人手动配置）、个人要能保留私人偏好（不影响他人）——这三个需求必须有三个不同的层级来分别满足，否则任意一个需求就无法实现。结论是：这种"对个人用户透明、对企业用户完整"的分层设计是合理的，但用户文档应该明确告诉个人用户"你只需要关心这三层"，而不是展示全部九层的复杂性。后者会造成不必要的认知负担。
qa.aEn: A fair critique worth engaging. For solo developers, five of the nine layers (the four sub-layers of Policy Settings + Feature Flag) are essentially invisible and don't need attention. The files you actually need to configure are usually just two or three: `~/.claude/settings.json` (global preferences), `.claude/settings.json` (project config), and `CLAUDE.md` (AI behavior instructions). So the complexity impact on individual users is limited — you can live in three layers forever, and the rest stays invisible. But the nine layers aren't "designed for individual users" — they're "indispensable for enterprise users." In enterprise scenarios: IT needs to enforce security policies (un-bypassable by employees), teams need to share project conventions (without each member configuring manually), and individuals need to keep personal preferences (without affecting others). These three needs require three distinct layers — otherwise any one of them can't be met. Conclusion: the "transparent to individuals, complete for enterprises" layered design is reasonable, but user documentation should explicitly tell individual users "you only need to care about these three layers" instead of parading all nine's complexity. The latter creates unnecessary cognitive overhead.

qa.q: Claude Code 的终端界面是怎么实现的？命令行窗口里的"按钮"和"对话框"是怎么做到的？
qa.qEn: How is Claude Code's terminal UI implemented? How do you get "buttons" and "dialogs" inside a command-line window?
qa.a: 这是一个让很多人感到惊讶的技术事实：Claude Code 的终端界面本质上是一个 React 应用，只不过渲染到了终端，而不是浏览器。普通浏览器里的 React 把组件树渲染成 HTML DOM，浏览器把 HTML 变成可见的网页。Claude Code 里的 React 把组件树渲染成 ANSI 转义序列（终端用来显示颜色和控制光标位置的特殊字符串），终端解析这些序列后显示出带颜色的文字、边框、进度条等视觉效果。这个把 React 翻译到终端的框架叫做 Ink，Claude Code 对其进行了深度 fork（96 个文件，19,842 行代码）。你在 Claude Code 里看到的一切——流式文字输出、权限确认弹窗、工具调用展示、进度指示器——都是这个 React 渲染管线的产物。通俗比喻：就像在记事本窗口里运行了一个完整的图形应用——容器是纯文字界面，但里面运行的是有组件、有状态、有事件处理的完整应用程序。
qa.aEn: Here's a technical fact that surprises many people: **Claude Code's terminal UI is essentially a React application** — just rendered to a terminal instead of a browser. In a normal browser, React renders a component tree to HTML DOM, which the browser displays as a web page. In Claude Code, React renders the component tree to **ANSI escape sequences** — the special strings terminals use to show colors and control cursor position. The terminal parses these sequences and displays colored text, borders, progress bars, and other visuals. The framework that translates React to the terminal is **Ink**, which Claude Code has deeply forked (96 files, 19,842 lines). Everything you see in Claude Code — streaming text output, permission-confirmation dialogs, tool-call displays, progress indicators — is a product of this React rendering pipeline. Analogy: it's like running a full graphical application inside a Notepad window — the container is a plain-text interface, but inside it runs a complete application with components, state, and event handling.

qa.q: 为什么要 Fork Ink 而不是直接用原版？这 19,842 行代码是在做什么？
qa.qEn: Why fork Ink instead of using the original? What are those 19,842 lines of code doing?
qa.a: 原版 Ink 是一个轻量级框架，设计目标是帮开发者用 React 写简单的 CLI——进度条、交互式表单、颜色化输出。但 Claude Code 的 UI 需求远超"简单 CLI"：流式文字渲染（AI 一个字一个字输出，界面要实时更新）、虚拟滚动（对话很长时，只渲染屏幕可见部分，节省性能）、鼠标支持（点击、拖拽、文字选择）、全文搜索（在对话历史里搜索关键词并高亮）、完整的焦点管理（Tab 键在不同输入框间切换）。原版 Ink 不支持这些。更关键的是，原版 Ink 使用了一个 WASM 版本的布局引擎（yoga-wasm），这带来了三个问题：启动延迟（WASM 需要异步加载和编译）、内存泄漏（WASM 线性内存只增不减）、并发安全问题（React 并发模式下的 use-after-free 漏洞）。Claude Code 把布局引擎改成了纯 TypeScript 实现，一次性解决了三个问题。19,842 行代码主要是：纯 TS Yoga 布局引擎（CSS Flexbox 计算）、双缓冲屏幕渲染（类似游戏引擎的双缓冲技术）、完整的鼠标和事件系统、文字选择系统、终端 I/O 原语。这不是代码膨胀，而是为了特定功能需求做的完整工程投入。
qa.aEn: The upstream Ink is a lightweight framework designed to help developers write simple CLIs with React — progress bars, interactive forms, colorized output. But Claude Code's UI needs go far beyond "simple CLI": streaming text rendering (AI outputs character-by-character, the UI updates in real time); virtual scrolling (for long conversations, only render the visible portion to save performance); mouse support (click, drag, text selection); full-text search (search and highlight keywords in conversation history); complete focus management (Tab to cycle between input boxes). Upstream Ink doesn't support these. More critically, upstream Ink uses a WASM-based layout engine (yoga-wasm), which brings three problems: startup latency (WASM requires async loading and compilation), memory leaks (WASM linear memory only grows), and concurrency-safety issues (use-after-free bugs under React's concurrent mode). Claude Code rewrote the layout engine in pure TypeScript, solving all three at once. The 19,842 lines mainly cover: a pure-TS Yoga layout engine (CSS Flexbox calculation), double-buffered screen rendering (similar to game-engine double buffering), a complete mouse and event system, a text-selection system, and terminal I/O primitives. Not bloat — complete engineering investment for specific feature needs.

qa.q: 双缓冲技术在终端 UI 里是什么意思？和游戏里的双缓冲有什么关系？
qa.qEn: What does double buffering mean in a terminal UI? How does it relate to double buffering in games?
qa.a: 双缓冲是一个经典的渲染优化技术，游戏和终端 UI 里的原理完全相同。问题场景：每次更新界面（比如 AI 输出了新字符），直接在屏幕上修改会产生"撕裂"效果——用户可能在某一帧看到一半旧内容一半新内容，因为渲染过程不是瞬时完成的。双缓冲解决方案：维护两个"画布"——前台缓冲区（用户当前看到的内容）和后台缓冲区（正在构建的下一帧）。每次更新在后台缓冲区计算完整的新画面，计算完毕后一次性"翻转"——把后台变成前台。用户只会看到完整的新画面，不会看到中间状态。Claude Code 的实现（`screen.ts`，1,486 行）：前台缓冲区存储当前显示状态，后台缓冲区存储下一帧；渲染时只发送两个缓冲区之间的"差异"（哪些字符改变了），而不是重绘整个屏幕——这对终端来说尤其重要，因为 ANSI 转义序列的传输量直接影响终端响应速度。还有一个"字符池"（Character Pool）优化：复用频繁出现的字符对象，减少内存分配和垃圾回收，这和游戏引擎里的对象池（Object Pool）模式完全一致。
qa.aEn: **Double buffering** is a classic rendering optimization; the principle is identical in games and terminal UIs. **Problem**: each time you update the UI (say, the AI emits a new character), directly modifying the screen produces "tearing" — users may see half-old, half-new content in one frame because rendering isn't instantaneous. **Double-buffering solution**: maintain two "canvases" — a **front buffer** (what the user currently sees) and a **back buffer** (the next frame being built). Each update computes the complete new image in the back buffer, then flips them all at once — back becomes front. The user only ever sees complete frames, never an intermediate state. Claude Code's implementation (`screen.ts`, 1,486 lines): the front buffer stores the current display state, the back buffer stores the next frame. When rendering, only the **diff** between the two buffers is sent (which characters changed) — not a full redraw. This is especially important for terminals, because the volume of ANSI escape sequences transmitted directly affects terminal responsiveness. There's also a **Character Pool** optimization: reuse frequently occurring character objects to reduce memory allocation and garbage collection — identical to the **Object Pool** pattern in game engines.

qa.q: 在终端里运行 React 是不是有点用力过猛？aider 用纯文本输出就工作得很好，为什么 Claude Code 要搞这么复杂的 UI 系统？
qa.qEn: Isn't running React in a terminal overkill? Aider works fine with plain text output — why does Claude Code need this complex UI system?
qa.a: 这是一个公平的质疑，值得认真评估。Aider 的方案：纯 ANSI 着色的文本输出，没有任何 UI 框架。确实工作得很好——对于核心用例（和 AI 讨论代码、应用修改），简洁的文本输出完全够用。Claude Code 选择复杂 UI 的理由：工具调用可视化——当 Claude 同时执行多个工具时，需要清晰地展示"正在读取哪个文件"、"Bash 命令执行了多久"等状态，纯文本流很难表达并发信息；权限确认界面——用户需要对工具调用快速作出 Allow/Deny 决策，一个结构化的弹窗比一行文字更直观；流式渲染的视觉质量——AI 输出的代码块需要语法高亮，差异对比（diff）需要颜色区分增删行，这些在纯文本下很难保持视觉清晰；长对话的浏览体验——虚拟滚动和搜索功能让用户能在长对话历史里快速定位，纯文本输出只能靠终端的自然滚动。但质疑也有道理：这 19,842 行 UI 代码是真实的维护成本。如果 Anthropic 的工程力量有限，这些投入在功能上未必比"改进 AI 能力或添加更多工具"的投资回报率更高。这最终是一个产品哲学问题：Claude Code 定位是"精致的日常工具"还是"功能够用的实用工具"。
qa.aEn: A fair critique worth serious evaluation. **Aider's approach**: plain ANSI-colored text output, no UI framework. It works fine — for the core use case (discuss code with AI, apply edits), concise text output is enough. **Claude Code's reasons for the complex UI**: **tool-call visualization** — when Claude runs multiple tools simultaneously, you need to clearly display "which file is being read," "how long has this Bash command been running" — a plain text stream can't really convey concurrency. **Permission-confirmation interface** — users need to make Allow/Deny decisions quickly; a structured dialog is more intuitive than a line of text. **Streaming visual quality** — AI-output code blocks need syntax highlighting, diffs need colors to distinguish additions and deletions — hard to keep visually clear in plain text. **Long-conversation browsing** — virtual scrolling and search let users quickly locate content in long histories; plain text can only rely on the terminal's native scrolling. But the critique has merit: those 19,842 UI lines are real maintenance cost. If Anthropic's engineering capacity is limited, this investment may not yield a higher ROI than "improving AI capability or adding more tools." Ultimately it's a product-philosophy question: is Claude Code positioned as a "polished daily tool" or a "sufficiently functional utility"?

qa.q: 什么是"横切关注点"？为什么要单独用一章来讲这些？
qa.qEn: What are "cross-cutting concerns"? Why a dedicated chapter for them?
qa.a: 横切关注点（Cross-Cutting Concerns）是软件工程中的一个概念，指那些不属于任何单一功能模块，但却在整个系统里到处出现的通用机制。举个生活例子：餐厅有厨房（做菜）、前台（接待）、仓库（存货）这些功能区，但"食品安全检查"和"财务记录"不属于任何一个区，而是在所有区域都要执行的"横切"逻辑。Claude Code 的横切关注点包括 12 个，比如：Feature Flag（功能开关）——任何一个模块都可能被某个 Flag 控制；错误处理和重试——任何模块都可能出错，都需要相似的重试逻辑；遥测（使用数据收集）——每个操作都需要被统计；国际化——每个界面文字都需要考虑语言；认证和会话管理——每次请求都需要带认证信息。这些内容如果分散在各个章节里讲，会造成重复和碎片化；但它们又不适合归入某个单一模块。单独一章"横切关注点"是软件架构文档的标准做法——把这些"系统粘合剂"集中在一处讲清楚。
qa.aEn: **Cross-cutting concerns** is a software-engineering concept for mechanisms that don't belong to any single functional module but show up everywhere across the system. Life example: a restaurant has functional zones (kitchen, reception, storeroom), but "food safety inspection" and "financial bookkeeping" don't belong to any zone — they're cross-cutting logic executed across all zones. Claude Code has 12 cross-cutting concerns, including: **Feature Flags** — any module might be gated by some flag. **Error handling and retries** — any module can fail and needs similar retry logic. **Telemetry** — every operation needs to be counted. **Internationalization** — every UI string has to consider language. **Auth and session management** — every request has to carry auth info. If you scattered these across chapters, you'd get repetition and fragmentation — but they don't naturally belong to any one module either. A dedicated "cross-cutting concerns" chapter is the standard practice in architecture documentation — explain these "system glue" pieces in one place.

qa.q: Feature Flag 是什么？为什么 Claude Code 要在代码里藏这么多"隐藏功能"？
qa.qEn: What are Feature Flags? Why does Claude Code hide so many "hidden features" in its code?
qa.a: Feature Flag（功能开关）是一种软件开发实践：功能代码已经写好并打包进产品，但通过一个开关控制它是否对外可见。就像餐厅的隐藏菜单——菜已经研发好，厨师也会做，但只对特定顾客开放。Claude Code 使用了编译期 Feature Flag——Bun 打包时，`feature('FLAG_NAME')` 被替换为字面量 `true` 或 `false`。如果是 `false`，整段代码（包括相关模块的导入）被 Dead Code Elimination 完全移除，不会出现在发布给用户的文件里。已知的编译期 Flag 包括：`TEAMMEM`（团队记忆功能）、`VOICE_MODE`（语音模式）、`BASH_CLASSIFIER`（Bash 命令安全分类器）、`QUICK_SEARCH`（快速搜索）等。为什么这样做？一是渐进式发布——新功能先在内部（Anthropic 员工的 ant build）完整测试，确认稳定后再在外部版本开启；二是零运行时开销——关闭的功能根本不存在于产品中，不占用内存，不影响启动速度；三是 A/B 测试支持——可以对不同用户群体开启不同 Flag，观察效果差异。对用户来说，某些功能（比如语音模式）可能在特定版本才开放，这正是 Feature Flag 的设计意图。
qa.aEn: **Feature Flags** are a development practice: feature code is written and bundled into the product, but a switch controls whether it's externally visible — like a restaurant's secret menu: the dishes are developed and the chef can cook them, but only specific customers can order them. Claude Code uses **compile-time Feature Flags** — during Bun bundling, `feature('FLAG_NAME')` is replaced with a literal `true` or `false`. If it's `false`, the entire enclosed code (including related module imports) is completely removed by Dead Code Elimination and never appears in user-shipped files. Known compile-time flags include: `TEAMMEM` (team memory), `VOICE_MODE` (voice mode), `BASH_CLASSIFIER` (Bash command safety classifier), `QUICK_SEARCH` (quick search), and more. Why do this? **Progressive rollout**: new features are fully tested internally first (Anthropic employees' `ant build`) and enabled in external versions only when stable. **Zero runtime overhead**: disabled features don't exist in the product — no memory, no startup cost. **A/B testing support**: different flags for different user cohorts to observe impact. For users, certain features (e.g., voice mode) may only open up in specific versions — that's literally the intent of Feature Flags.

qa.q: Claude Code 的错误重试机制是怎样的？API 调用失败后会无限重试吗？
qa.qEn: How does Claude Code's error-retry mechanism work? Does it retry API calls forever?
qa.a: 不会无限重试，而是使用了"指数退避 + 最大重试次数"的经典策略。指数退避：第一次失败等 1 秒重试，还是失败等 2 秒，再失败等 4 秒，以此类推——每次等待时间翻倍。这避免了在服务器压力大时雪上加霜（如果所有失败的请求立刻重试，会进一步加重负载）。最大重试次数：超过上限后，抛出 `CannotRetryError`，告诉调用方"重试已耗尽"，不再继续。不同错误的重试策略不同：429（Rate Limit，超出频率限制）：总是重试，等待服务器指定的 retry-after 时间；500（服务器内部错误）：可能重试，取决于具体错误内容；401（认证失败）：不重试，认证错误重试也没用，应该直接报告给用户；400（参数错误）：不重试，是客户端问题，重试还是会失败。模型降级：如果主模型（比如 claude-opus-4）连续失败多次，系统会触发 `FallbackTriggeredError`，自动切换到备用模型（比如 claude-sonnet）继续工作，对用户透明。这种"优雅降级"比直接报错更好——任务可能因为用了低一档的模型而质量稍低，但总比完全中断好。
qa.aEn: No — it uses the classic "exponential backoff + max retries" strategy. **Exponential backoff**: wait 1 second after the first failure, 2 seconds after the second, 4 seconds after the third — doubling each time. This avoids piling on when the server is stressed (if every failed request retried immediately, the load would get worse). **Max retries**: once the cap is reached, throw `CannotRetryError`, telling the caller "retries exhausted" — no further attempts. Different errors, different strategies. **429 (rate limit exceeded)**: always retry, waiting the server's specified `retry-after` time. **500 (server internal error)**: possibly retry, depending on the specific error. **401 (auth failed)**: don't retry — retrying an auth error is pointless; report directly to the user. **400 (bad parameters)**: don't retry — it's a client issue, retries will fail identically. **Model downgrade**: if the primary model (e.g., claude-opus-4) fails repeatedly, the system triggers `FallbackTriggeredError` and automatically switches to a backup model (e.g., claude-sonnet) to continue, transparent to the user. This **graceful degradation** beats hard failing — the task may run at slightly lower quality with a lower-tier model, but it's better than complete interruption.

qa.q: Dream Mode 是什么？这个名字听起来很神秘——AI 在"做梦"吗？
qa.qEn: What is Dream Mode? The name sounds mysterious — is the AI "dreaming"?
qa.a: Dream Mode 是 Claude Code 代码里出现的一个特殊任务类型，文档注释中暗示这是某种"睡眠期记忆整合"的类比。从代码结构看，`dream` 作为一种任务类型出现在 `Task.ts` 的类型定义中，但相关代码在公开泄露版本中状态未完全确认，可能是一个实验性功能。从概念上理解，"睡眠期记忆整合"对应的功能可能是：在用户不活跃期间，系统对之前的对话历史、项目代码、Memory 条目进行后台分析，提取更高层次的洞见和模式，更新 Memory 系统。就像人类睡眠时大脑整合白天的记忆，把短期记忆转化为长期记忆，并形成新的连接和洞见。这类似于 Auto-Memory（自动记忆提取）的增强版：不只是在对话结束时提取事实，而是在更长时间维度上分析模式。目前这个功能的具体实现和开放状态尚不明朗。但这个名字本身透露了 Anthropic 在记忆和持久化方向上的雄心——他们在思考如何让 Claude 有"真正的记忆"，而不只是会话内的上下文。
qa.aEn: **Dream Mode** is a special task type that appears in Claude Code's code; the docstrings suggest an analogy to "sleep-phase memory consolidation." Structurally, `dream` appears as a task type in `Task.ts`'s type definitions, but in the publicly leaked version its related code is not fully confirmed — it may be an experimental feature. Conceptually, "sleep-phase memory consolidation" likely corresponds to: during periods when the user is inactive, the system analyzes prior conversation history, project code, and Memory entries in the background — extracting higher-level insights and patterns, and updating the Memory system. Like the human brain during sleep, which consolidates daytime memories, converts short-term to long-term memory, and forms new connections and insights. This is like an enhanced version of Auto-Memory: not just extracting facts at conversation end, but analyzing patterns over a longer time horizon. The feature's concrete implementation and release status aren't fully clear. But the name itself reveals Anthropic's ambition around memory and persistence — they're thinking about how to give Claude "real memory" rather than just in-session context.

qa.q: 遥测（使用数据收集）功能意味着什么？Claude Code 在默默记录我的操作吗？
qa.qEn: What does telemetry (usage-data collection) mean? Is Claude Code silently recording my actions?
qa.a: Claude Code 确实有遥测系统（源码中可以看到 `telemetry` 相关代码），用于收集使用数据帮助 Anthropic 改进产品。透明度方面，Claude Code 在首次启动时会告知用户并请求同意遥测，你可以选择关闭。通常收集的是**匿名使用统计**：功能使用频率（哪个工具被调用最多）、错误发生率（哪类操作最容易出错）、会话时长、系统性能指标等。不会收集的（至少按照通常做法）：对话内容、代码内容、文件路径、个人身份信息。但"不会收集"依赖于 Anthropic 对自己隐私政策的遵守，用户无法独立验证。对于有严格隐私要求的企业用户：Claude Code 支持企业级的数据本地化部署，遥测可以被策略关闭；企业内部的 API 代理服务器可以拦截所有外部通信。实践建议：查阅最新的 Anthropic 隐私政策（anthropic.com/privacy），了解数据收集和使用的具体条款。如果你在处理高度敏感的代码（军事、医疗、金融核心系统），谨慎评估是否适合使用基于云 API 的 AI 工具，不只是 Claude Code，这是对所有同类工具的通用建议。
qa.aEn: Claude Code does have a telemetry system (you can see `telemetry`-related code in the source) that collects usage data to help Anthropic improve the product. **On transparency**: Claude Code tells you about it on first launch and asks for telemetry consent — you can opt out. What's typically collected is **anonymous usage statistics**: feature usage frequency (which tools are called most), error rates (which operations fail most often), session duration, system performance metrics, and similar. What's typically **not** collected: conversation content, code content, file paths, personally identifiable information. But "not collected" relies on Anthropic following its own privacy policy — users can't independently verify this. For privacy-strict enterprise users: Claude Code supports enterprise-level data-localization deployments where telemetry can be policy-disabled, and the enterprise's internal API proxy can intercept all outbound communication. Practical advice: check Anthropic's latest privacy policy (anthropic.com/privacy) for the specific data-collection terms. If you're handling highly sensitive code (military, medical, financial core systems), carefully evaluate whether cloud-API-based AI tools are appropriate — not just Claude Code. This advice applies to every tool in the category.
